_**Agent (gpt-5.2-codex)**_

Your Python is managed by Homebrew, so system-wide `pip` installs are blocked. The safe fix is to use a virtual environment.

Run these commands in the project folder that contains `api_server.py`:

```
cd "/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/html"
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python3 -m pip install flask
```

Then start the server again in that same terminal (so the venv stays active). If you want, tell me the exact start command and I’ll run it for you.

_**Agent (gpt-5.2-codex)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
**Explaining venv and requirements.txt**

I'll explain that a virtual environment (venv) isolates dependencies per project, recommend creating it once, and ensuring the same Python interpreter is used. I'll also advise generating requirements.txt with `pip freeze > requirements.txt` to record dependencies consistently.</details></think>

_**Agent (gpt-5.2-codex)**_

Not if you keep using the same virtual environment. The installs persist inside `.venv`, but they won’t be available if you run with a different Python or in a new venv.

To make it stick and reproducible, create a `requirements.txt` once:

```
cd "/Users/katespurr/Desktop/Halifax Now/html"
source .venv/bin/activate
python3 -m pip freeze > requirements.txt
```

Then in the future you can rebuild with:

```
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
```

If you want, I can generate the `requirements.txt` for you.

---

  "plan": "# Ignore Missing Audit Events\n\n## Approach\n- Add a persistent ignore list file (seeded from the current missing set) and filter missing events against it during the audit comparison step.\n- Ensure audit outputs used by the dashboard are generated from the filtered list so the “Missing from Site” count drops accordingly.\n\n## Files to Update\n- [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/compare_site_xml_to_master.py&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/compare_site_xml_to_master.py)\n  - Add loading of an ignore list (by date/time/title key) and filter &#96;only_master&#96; before writing outputs.\n  - Add a &#96;--seed-ignore-missing&#96; option to create the ignore list from the current missing set.\n  - Write &#96;audit_future_only_in_master.csv&#96; and &#96;audit_master_only_by_venue.csv&#96; from the filtered missing events so the dashboard reflects the ignore list.\n- [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/run_all_with_audit.command&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/run_all_with_audit.command)\n  - If the ignore list doesn’t exist, call the compare step with &#96;--seed-ignore-missing&#96; once to auto-seed from the current missing set.\n- (Optional) [&#96;/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/AUDIT_PROCESS_OVERVIEW.md&#96;](/Users/katespurr/Desktop/halifax_event_scrapers_v3/AUDIT_PROCESS_OVERVIEW.md)\n  - Document the ignore list behavior and how to reset it if desired.\n\n## Output/Behavior Changes\n- New file: &#96;audit_ignore_missing.csv&#96; (seeded automatically on first run).\n- Missing counts and “ready_to_import_from_audit.csv” exclude ignored events.\n- &#96;audit_dashboard.html&#96; reflects the filtered missing count without extra manual steps.\n\n## Todos\n- Add ignore-list read/filter + seed option in &#96;compare_site_xml_to_master.py&#96;.\n- Update &#96;run_all_with_audit.command&#96;
